title: GDPR Overview and Scope
content: GDPR applies to any organization processing personal data of EU residents regardless of where the organization is located. Personal data = any info relating to identified or identifiable natural person. Key obligations: lawful basis; data subject rights; DPO appointment; breach notification; DPIA for high-risk processing.

title: GDPR 8 Data Subject Rights Requirements
content: Eight rights: 1.Right to be informed (privacy notice); 2.Right of access (within 30 days); 3.Right to rectification; 4.Right to erasure (right to be forgotten); 5.Right to restrict processing; 6.Right to data portability (machine-readable format); 7.Right to object; 8.Rights related to automated decision-making. System must support all 8.
tags: gdpr rights data-subject erasure portability access

title: GDPR Consent Requirements Pattern
content: Valid consent must be: freely given (no bundled consent); specific (per purpose); informed (clear plain language); unambiguous (affirmative action — no pre-ticked boxes); withdrawable (as easy to withdraw as to give). Store consent record: who/when/what/how. Re-consent required if purpose changes.

title: GDPR Data Processing Agreement Requirements
content: DPA requirements between controller and processor: subject matter and duration of processing; nature and purpose; type of personal data and categories of data subjects; obligations of processor (process only on instructions; confidentiality; security measures; sub-processor approval; assist with rights requests; delete/return data on termination).

title: GDPR DPIA Requirements
content: DPIA required when: large-scale processing of sensitive data; systematic profiling; processing of public area data (CCTV at scale). DPIA content: description of processing; necessity and proportionality assessment; risk assessment; risk mitigation measures. Must be done before processing begins.

title: GDPR Breach Notification Requirements
content: Data breach notification: to supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware (if risk to individuals); to affected individuals without undue delay (if high risk). Notification content: nature of breach; categories and approximate number of individuals/records affected; likely consequences; measures taken.

title: GDPR Data Retention Requirements Pattern
content: Data retention requirements: define retention period per data category; legal basis for retention period; deletion or anonymization process after retention expires; retention schedule reviewed annually; backups purged per schedule; audit log of deletions; exceptions for legal hold.

title: GDPR Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten)
content: Right to erasure applies when: data no longer necessary; consent withdrawn; objection upheld; unlawfully processed. System requirements: erasure within 30 days; cascade to processors/third parties; anonymization as alternative to deletion; audit trail of erasure; exceptions for legal obligation/public interest.

title: GDPR Data Portability Requirements
content: Data portability: provide personal data in structured/commonly used/machine-readable format (JSON/CSV); direct transfer to another controller on request; applies only to data provided by subject and processed by consent or contract; response within 30 days.

title: PCI-DSS 12 Requirements Summary
content: PCI-DSS 12 requirements: 1.Network security controls; 2.Secure configurations; 3.Protect stored account data; 4.Protect data in transit (TLS); 5.Protect against malware; 6.Secure software development; 7.Restrict access by need-to-know; 8.Identify users and authenticate; 9.Restrict physical access; 10.Log and monitor; 11.Test security; 12.Security policy.

title: PCI-DSS Encryption Requirements
content: PCI-DSS encryption: cardholder data encrypted at rest (AES-256); transmission over open networks encrypted (TLS 1.2+ minimum); PAN masked when displayed (first 6/last 4 digits max); no PAN in logs; encryption keys managed separately from encrypted data; key rotation annually.
tags: pci-dss encryption pan cardholder-data tls aes

title: PCI-DSS Access Control Requirements
content: PCI-DSS access: unique ID for each user (no shared accounts); MFA for all non-console administrative access; MFA for all remote access; access restricted to need-to-know; vendor access enabled only when needed and monitored; access review quarterly.

title: PCI-DSS Audit Trail Requirements
content: Audit log requirements: log all access to cardholder data; all admin actions; all access to audit logs; invalid access attempts; authentication events. Retention: 12 months minimum (3 months immediately available). Log fields: user ID/date-time/event type/component/success or failure.

title: HIPAA PHI Definition and Scope
content: PHI (Protected Health Information): individually identifiable health information in any form (electronic/paper/oral). 18 HIPAA identifiers including: name; address; dates (except year); phone; SSN; medical record number; health plan number; device identifiers. De-identification requires removing all 18 or expert statistical method.
tags: hipaa phi definition identifiers healthcare compliance

title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Rule
content: Minimum necessary: covered entities must make reasonable efforts to limit PHI disclosure to minimum necessary for purpose. Requirements: role-based access to PHI; access justified by job function; bulk queries to PHI require approval; audit PHI access; workforce training on minimum necessary.

title: HIPAA Business Associate Agreement Requirements
content: BAA required when vendor handles PHI. BAA must include: permitted uses/disclosures; safeguard requirements (HIPAA Security Rule); sub-contractor BAA requirement; breach notification obligation (within 60 days); return/destroy PHI on termination; cooperation with compliance reviews.

title: HIPAA Security Rule Requirements
content: HIPAA Security Rule safeguards: Administrative (risk analysis; workforce training; contingency plan); Physical (facility access controls; workstation controls; device controls); Technical (access control; audit controls; integrity controls; transmission security). Annual risk assessment required.
tags: hipaa security-rule safeguards administrative physical technical

title: SOX Financial Controls Requirements
content: SOX compliance requirements: segregation of duties for financial transactions (initiator != approver); complete audit trail for financial data changes; change management controls; access controls to financial systems; quarterly management assessment; annual external audit. IT General Controls (ITGC) scope.

title: SOX Audit Trail Requirements
content: SOX audit trail: all financial record changes logged with user/timestamp/old value/new value; logs immutable (cannot be altered or deleted); access to financial data logged; privileged access monitored; log retention 7 years; logs reviewed regularly; anomaly detection.
tags: sox audit-trail financial immutable retention compliance

title: OWASP Top 10 2021 - BA Requirements Checklist
content: OWASP Top 10 requirements: A01 Broken Access Control (define authorization matrix); A02 Cryptographic Failures (specify encryption for data at rest/transit); A03 Injection (input validation requirements); A04 Insecure Design (threat modeling); A05 Security Misconfiguration (hardening requirements); A06 Vulnerable Components (dependency policy); A07 Auth Failures (MFA/session requirements).
tags: owasp top-10 security requirements ba checklist

title: OWASP A01 Broken Access Control Requirements
content: Access control requirements: deny by default; access control checks server-side (not client-side); per-resource authorization checks (not just page-level); JWT validation on every request; admin functions require explicit admin role; rate limit API to prevent enumeration; log access failures.

title: OWASP A03 Injection Prevention Requirements
content: Injection prevention requirements: all user input validated and sanitized server-side; parameterized queries for all database operations (no dynamic SQL); ORM usage preferred; HTML encoding for output; command execution inputs validated against allowlist; file paths sanitized; error messages do not reveal system internals.
tags: owasp injection sql xss input-validation requirements

title: OWASP A07 Authentication Failure Requirements
content: Authentication requirements: MFA required for sensitive operations; account lockout after 5 failed attempts; session timeout (15 min idle/8 hour absolute); secure session token (httpOnly/Secure/SameSite cookie); password requirements (12+ chars/complexity); breach password check; reauthentication for sensitive actions.
tags: owasp authentication mfa session password requirements

title: WCAG 2.1 AA - Perceivable Principle Requirements
content: Perceivable requirements: text alternatives for all non-text content; captions for pre-recorded video; audio description for video; content adaptable (works without color/shape/size); color not sole means of conveying info; minimum contrast ratio 4.5:1 for normal text; 3:1 for large text; no content flashing > 3 times per second.
tags: wcag perceivable accessibility a11y contrast captions

title: WCAG 2.1 AA - Operable Principle Requirements
content: Operable requirements: all functionality keyboard accessible; no keyboard trap; skip navigation link; page titled; focus order logical; link purpose clear from context; no timing restrictions (or adjustable); pause/stop/hide for moving content; no seizure-inducing content; focus visible (2:1 contrast minimum).
tags: wcag operable keyboard focus accessibility requirements

title: WCAG 2.1 AA - Understandable Principle Requirements
content: Understandable requirements: page language declared; language of parts declared; no unexpected context changes on focus; labels for all form inputs; error identification in text; error suggestion provided; error prevention for legal/financial (review/confirm/undo); consistent navigation across pages.
tags: wcag understandable language forms error accessibility

title: WCAG 2.1 AA - Robust Principle Requirements
content: Robust requirements: valid HTML/CSS; name/role/value programmatically determinable for all UI components; ARIA roles and attributes used correctly; status messages programmatically determinable; compatible with current and future assistive technologies; test with actual screen readers (NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver).
tags: wcag robust aria html assistive-technology requirements

title: Data Residency Requirements Pattern
content: Data residency requirements: specify geographic regions where data may be stored and processed; cloud provider regions allowed; cross-border transfer restrictions; list of approved third-party countries; standard contractual clauses (SCCs) for transfers; data sovereignty clause in contracts; documentation for compliance audit.

title: Privacy by Design 7 Principles
content: Privacy by Design principles: 1.Proactive not reactive; 2.Privacy as default; 3.Privacy embedded into design; 4.Full functionality (positive-sum not zero-sum); 5.End-to-end security; 6.Visibility and transparency; 7.Respect for user privacy. BA must embed these in requirements from project start.

title: Cookie Consent Requirements (ePrivacy)
content: Cookie consent requirements: inform users of all cookies before setting; obtain explicit consent for non-essential cookies; no pre-ticked boxes; granular consent per category (analytics/marketing/functional); easy withdrawal mechanism; consent log stored; re-consent if purpose changes; essential cookies exempt.
tags: cookie consent eprivacy gdpr requirements banner

title: Age Verification COPPA Requirements
content: COPPA (USA) requirements for services directed to children under 13: verifiable parental consent before collecting personal info; no behavioral advertising to children; data retention limits; parental access/deletion rights. GDPR Article 8: member state age 13-16 for consent. Requirement: age gate with verification mechanism.
tags: coppa gdpr age-verification children privacy requirements

title: Accessibility Audit Checklist
content: Accessibility audit checklist: automated scan (axe/WAVE — catches ~30-40% of issues); manual keyboard navigation test; screen reader test (NVDA/JAWS for Windows; VoiceOver for Mac/iOS); color contrast check; zoom to 200% test; form error identification test; document accessibility (PDF tagged); video captions review.
tags: accessibility audit checklist wcag screen-reader testing

title: Compliance Requirements Traceability
content: Compliance traceability: each compliance requirement linked to regulation article/section; compliance requirement traced to system requirement; system requirement traced to test case; test case result documented; evidence package for audit includes: requirements/test results/screenshots/sign-offs.

title: Data Classification Requirements
content: Data classification levels: Public (freely shareable — no restrictions); Internal (employee only — standard access controls); Confidential (business sensitive — role-based access/encryption at rest/no external sharing without approval); Restricted (highest sensitivity PHI/PAN/credentials — MFA/encryption/audit log/DLP).

title: GDPR Privacy Notice Requirements
content: Privacy notice must include: identity and contact of controller; DPO contact (if applicable); purposes and legal basis for processing; legitimate interests (if applicable); recipients/transfers; retention periods; all data subject rights; right to withdraw consent; right to complain to supervisory authority; automated decision-making info.
